prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: HOMO–LUMO (frontier-orbital) gap, eV
Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with def2-TZVP basis set (tier “standard”); exact four-center integrals (no density fitting)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (formaldehyde.xyz); single-point calculation – no re-optimization (DFT//user geometry)
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software backend: PySCF (via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill)
SCF convergence: converged in 8 cycles; gradient criteria not applicable (single point)
Warnings: no warnings reported
Live log: written during run to a timestamped *.out* file in the working directory (streamed by chemkit while executing the task).

Result
The computed Koopmans-style HOMO–LUMO gap of formaldehyde at B3LYP/def2-TZVP in the gas phase is:

Δε(HOMO–LUMO) = 5.869671192772918 eV

All numbers and orbitals (HOMO = −7.6523067 eV, LUMO = −1.7826355 eV; additional frontier orbitals included in the JSON) come directly from the chemkit frontier-orbitals result JSON referenced above.

Engine integrity verdict: trustworthy (all automated checks passed).

Provenance: computed (this work).
